fields.title: Enumeration - Identity and System Snapshot
fields.summary: Collect user, kernel and host information for baseline context.
fields.prerequisites: Low-privilege shell in an authorized lab machine.
fields.explanation: These commands quickly identify privilege level, kernel version and distro information.
fields.warnings: Do not run on systems you do not own or have explicit permission to test.

fields.title: Enumeration - Processes, Network and Mounted Filesystems
fields.summary: Map running services and interesting filesystems.
fields.commands:
ps aux --sort=-%mem | head
ss -tulpn
mount
lsblk
df -h
fields.explanation: Useful for finding exposed services, writable mounts and suspicious daemons.
fields.warnings: Service visibility may be restricted by namespace/container boundaries.

fields.title: SUID/SGID - Quick Discovery
fields.summary: Locate SUID/SGID binaries outside default paths.
fields.explanation: High-value binaries can provide privilege escalation if misconfigured.
fields.warnings: Large filesystems can make these scans slow.

fields.title: SUID/SGID - Prioritize GTFOBins Candidates
fields.summary: Filter discovered binaries against common escalation candidates.
fields.prerequisites: List of SUID binaries from previous step.
fields.explanation: Prioritization helps focus manual testing on binaries with known abuse paths.
fields.warnings: Presence alone does not guarantee exploitation success.

fields.title: Sudo Abuse - List Privileges
fields.summary: Inspect sudo rules available to the current user.
fields.prerequisites: User account with potential sudo permissions.
fields.explanation: The sudoers output reveals commands executable as root or other users.
fields.warnings: Repeated wrong password attempts may trigger lockouts or alerts.

fields.title: Sudo Abuse - Environment and Path Checks
fields.summary: Review sudo environment handling and potential path abuse vectors.
fields.explanation: Misconfigured env_keep/secure_path values can introduce escalation opportunities.
fields.warnings: Reading sudoers files may require privileges.

fields.title: Capabilities - Enumerate File Capabilities
fields.summary: Find binaries with elevated Linux capabilities.
fields.explanation: Capabilities can grant privileged operations without full root SUID.
fields.warnings: Output may be extensive on large hosts.

fields.title: Capabilities - Inspect Python/Perl Privileged Paths
fields.summary: Check if interpreters expose dangerous capabilities.
fields.explanation: Capability-enabled interpreters are often high-priority escalation vectors.
fields.warnings: Manual validation is required before any escalation attempt.

fields.title: Cron Jobs - Enumerate System and User Tasks
fields.summary: Inspect cron directories and crontabs for writable scripts.
fields.explanation: Weak cron script permissions can enable command injection or script replacement.
fields.warnings: User crontab may be empty depending on account.

fields.title: Cron Jobs - Writable Script Triage
fields.summary: Find world/group writable scripts referenced by cron.
fields.explanation: Writable scheduled scripts are critical findings in privilege escalation assessments.
fields.warnings: Do not modify files outside authorized lab scope.

fields.title: Path Hijacking - PATH Variable Review
fields.summary: Check PATH ordering and writable directories in execution flow.
fields.commands:
echo $PATH
for p in $(echo $PATH | tr ':' ' '); do ls -ld $p; done
fields.explanation: Writable directories early in PATH can allow command shadowing.
fields.warnings: Containerized shells may not reflect host PATH policies.

fields.title: Path Hijacking - Sudo Secure Path Analysis
fields.summary: Check secure_path and command calls without absolute paths.
fields.explanation: Misaligned secure_path and writable directories can lead to privilege escalation.
fields.warnings: sudoers file access may be restricted.

fields.title: TTY Upgrade - Python PTY Spawn
fields.summary: Upgrade non-interactive shell into a stable PTY session.
fields.commands:
python3 -c 'import pty; pty.spawn("/bin/bash")'
export TERM=xterm
stty rows 40 columns 120
fields.explanation: Improves usability for editors, su/sudo prompts, and signal handling.
fields.warnings: Terminal dimensions should match your local terminal.

fields.title: TTY Upgrade - Script Utility Method
fields.summary: Use script command to improve interactive shell behavior.
fields.explanation: Alternative to Python when interpreters are unavailable.
fields.warnings: Some minimal systems do not include script utility.

fields.title: File Transfer - Pull Files with Curl/Wget
fields.summary: Transfer tools from controlled infrastructure to target host.
fields.prerequisites: Outbound HTTP allowed in lab network.
fields.explanation: HTTP pull is often the simplest transfer method in lab environments.
fields.warnings: Replace ATTACKER_IP with your authorized lab host.

fields.title: File Transfer - Push Files with Netcat
fields.summary: Send and receive files over a raw TCP channel in lab setups.
fields.commands:
# Receiver
nc -lvnp 9001 > received.bin
# Sender
nc TARGET_IP 9001 < payload.bin
fields.explanation: Useful fallback when HTTP tools are unavailable.
fields.warnings: Traffic is plaintext; use only in controlled labs.
